untranslatable_reason: Frying Pan pattern requires multi-bar geometric shape recognition (downward-sloping handle at 30-45 degrees followed by upward breakout). This pattern detection requires trend line fitting and visual shape analysis that cannot be expressed using only the allowed point-in-time indicators and operators. The signal requires identifying a specific price formation geometry across multiple bars, which exceeds the capability of the available indicators (RSI, ATR, MA, Bollinger Bands, etc.). A working implementation would require a custom pattern recognition function or manual bar-by-bar chart analysis.
translation_notes: The Frying Pan is a classic technical pattern described by Bulkowski that involves: (1) a downward-sloping handle phase (30-45 degree angle), (2) followed by an upward breakout above the handle trend line. While volume clustering at formation start, bump start, and breakout is noted, high volume is not required. The entry is when price breaks above the handle trend line. To implement this signal accurately, a backtesting system would need a dedicated pattern scanner that can detect trend lines and breakout geometry, or the trader would need to manually identify these formations on a chart using arithmetic (not logarithmic) scale.
